programme.desc: Finden Sie heraus, wie die Aromen Chinas zu einem so festen Bestandteil der Küche Singapurs geworden sind.

programme.title: Xplore Cambodia
programme.desc: Sukhmani verliert ihr Herz an die unberührte Schönheit der weniger erforschten Inseln rund um die Strandstadt Sihanoukville.

programme.title: Off The Grid
programme.desc: Vom Stolpern über eine Statue von Guru Rinpoche bis zum Besuch eines lokalen Festivals in einem Kloster erforscht Alex den Einfluss des Buddhismus im Zanskar-Tal.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Sabrina Chakici kommt mit Budapest, der Hauptstadt Ungarns, auf Tuchfühlung und lernt ihre Geschichte und ihren Charakter kennen.

programme.title: Burn Appetit
programme.desc: Koch Jolly, ein indischer Küchenchef, der im Vereinigten Königreich lebt, möchte seine Liebe für gutes Essen und seine Leidenschaft für Motorräder auf einem epischen Trip durch Österreich verbinden.

programme.title: Bucket List
programme.desc: Daniel Coll & Paddy Doherty finden einige der besten Orte in Kyushu heraus, um ein lokales Schnaps auszuprobieren, und Bonito, eine lokale Delikatesse für Meeresfrüchte.

programme.title: Food Stories
programme.sub-title: Food Stories - Singapur – Die Mailaiischen Wurzeln
programme.desc: Finden Sie heraus, wie die Aromen Chinas zu einem so festen Bestandteil der Küche Singapurs geworden sind.

programme.title: Xplore Cambodia

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.sub-title: Gastronomie, Savoir Et Divertissement New Taipei City, Taiwan
programme.desc: À ne pas confondre avec la capitale Taipei, la nouvelle ville de Taipei est une immense étendue de terre qui entoure la capitale, englobant la majeure partie de la pointe nord de Taïwan. Alex Drobin découvre l'amour de la ville pour le baseball et la fondue, lorsqu'il teste ses compétences dans les cages des frappeurs, et savoure chaque minute pour découvrir tout ce qui se passe dans une fondue traditionnelle.

programme.title: Bliss
programme.desc: Qu'il s'agisse d'apprendre à cuisiner des spécialités traditionnelles et d'assister à une représentation du drame dansé le plus connu du Kerala, Kathakali, en passant par un traitement de spa inhabituel, développé à l'origine pour les combattants de Kalaripayattu, ses expériences lui offrent l'équilibre parfait entre culture et luxe.

programme.title: Best From The Rest
programme.sub-title: Le Top Du Top Oman 5 Attractions Incontournables
programme.desc: Un contraste saisissant de déserts, de montagnes et de plages, son paysage préservé, parsemé de grands forts, de palais exquis, de dunes ondulantes, de canyons et d'oueds luxuriants.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.sub-title: Le Moment Détente Park Hyatt, Maldives (Fr)
programme.desc: Un luxe inégalé, de superbes plages de sable blanc et un monde sous-marin incroyable font des Maldives l'escapade tropicale parfaite. Passionnée de voyages, Indeya Passfield cherche une expérience plus locale à Hadahaa, où elle fait une excursion dans le monde aquatique avec un biologiste marin. Elle s'essaye également à la confection de préparations de fruits de mer locaux et à la pêche en bateau traditionnel.

programme.sub-title: Le Moment Détente Park Hyatt, Maldives (Fr)
programme.desc: Un luxe inégalé, de superbes plages de sable blanc et un monde sous-marin incroyable font des Maldives l'escapade tropicale parfaite. Passionnée de voyages, Indeya Passfield cherche une expérience plus locale à Hadahaa, où elle fait une excursion dans le monde aquatique avec un biologiste marin. Elle s'essaye également à la confection de préparations de fruits de mer locaux et à la pêche en bateau traditionnel.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.sub-title: Gastronomie, Savoir Et Divertissement New Taipei City, Taiwan
programme.desc: À ne pas confondre avec la capitale Taipei, la nouvelle ville de Taipei est une immense étendue de terre qui entoure la capitale, englobant la majeure partie de la pointe nord de Taïwan. Alex Drobin découvre l'amour de la ville pour le baseball et la fondue, lorsqu'il teste ses compétences dans les cages des frappeurs, et savoure chaque minute pour découvrir tout ce qui se passe dans une fondue traditionnelle.

programme.title: Bliss
programme.desc: Qu'il s'agisse d'apprendre à cuisiner des spécialités traditionnelles et d'assister à une représentation du drame dansé le plus connu du Kerala, Kathakali, en passant par un traitement de spa inhabituel, développé à l'origine pour les combattants de Kalaripayattu, ses expériences lui offrent l'équilibre parfait entre culture et luxe.

programme.desc: Un contraste saisissant de déserts, de montagnes et de plages, son paysage préservé, parsemé de grands forts, de palais exquis, de dunes ondulantes, de canyons et d'oueds luxuriants.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.sub-title: Le Moment Détente Park Hyatt, Maldives (Fr)
programme.desc: Un luxe inégalé, de superbes plages de sable blanc et un monde sous-marin incroyable font des Maldives l'escapade tropicale parfaite. Passionnée de voyages, Indeya Passfield cherche une expérience plus locale à Hadahaa, où elle fait une excursion dans le monde aquatique avec un biologiste marin. Elle s'essaye également à la confection de préparations de fruits de mer locaux et à la pêche en bateau traditionnel.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.sub-title: Gastronomie, Savoir Et Divertissement New Taipei City, Taiwan
programme.desc: À ne pas confondre avec la capitale Taipei, la nouvelle ville de Taipei est une immense étendue de terre qui entoure la capitale, englobant la majeure partie de la pointe nord de Taïwan. Alex Drobin découvre l'amour de la ville pour le baseball et la fondue, lorsqu'il teste ses compétences dans les cages des frappeurs, et savoure chaque minute pour découvrir tout ce qui se passe dans une fondue traditionnelle.

programme.title: Bliss
programme.desc: Qu'il s'agisse d'apprendre à cuisiner des spécialités traditionnelles et d'assister à une représentation du drame dansé le plus connu du Kerala, Kathakali, en passant par un traitement de spa inhabituel, développé à l'origine pour les combattants de Kalaripayattu, ses expériences lui offrent l'équilibre parfait entre culture et luxe.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: Oslo is the oldest of the Scandinavian capitals; with a rich history dating back to over 1000 years, the city’s iconic monuments reveal its evolution through the ages to visitors. Elisha goes on a tour to see some of them such as the magnificent Arkeshus Castle, a medieval castle that was built to protect Oslo but was used instead as a prison, and Oslo City Hall, the administrative center of the city which hosts the annual Nobel peace prize ceremony.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: On a trip to Rajasthan, Alex meets Ajayraj Singh, a blue-blooded dignitary, who introduces her to the secret meat dishes of the kingdom of Mewar.

programme.title: Timeless Tamil Nadu
programme.desc: In the last leg of her journey in Tamil Nadu, Alex visits one of India’s southernmost towns, Rameshwaram.

programme.title: Xplore
programme.desc: The ancient temples of Sirpur to discover some of the most well preserved icons of Chhattisgarh's past.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Visit Belgrade, with Heather as she explores the city, its architecture and learns about its past by visiting historical monuments built during the Roman, Hungarian and Turkish era.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: Perhaps the most famous period in Norwegian history was the Viking Age. Far from just barbaric, axe-wielding invaders, the Vikings were Norse seafarers who left a major impact on European history through trade, colonization, and far-flung exploration. During Elisha’s visit to Oslo, she finds herself in awe of the extraordinary well-designed Viking ships at a museum, where she comes to know fascinating stories about the lives led by the Vikings.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: Alex meets Anjali Singh, a member of the royal family of Jaipur, who introduces her to two prized dishes of the city’s culinary heritage.

programme.title: The Savvy Nomad Philippines
programme.desc: Emeline explores Manila's historic Intramuros, where she gawks at colonial relics before diving into the city's vibrant chaos aboard a jeepney.

programme.title: Xplore
programme.desc: Kanger Wildlife National Park, Kutumsar Caves, Chitrakote are few places one should visit while on trip to Chattisgarh.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Heather explores Novi Sad, the financial and industrial hub of Serbia. Starting with canoeing on Danube and later exploring the city and its landmarks.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: In Bihar’s countryside, Alex gets acquainted with the earthy cuisine that fuels farmers to toil in the fields all day.

programme.title: Xplore
programme.desc: Handicrafts, Dokra art and traditional clay mask from the Kumhars are few thing, we did at Bastar, Chattisgarh.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: Catch the beauty of Prague through its fascinating history, its classic mix of Gothic and Renaissance architecture and more.

programme.title: The Savvy Nomad Philippines
programme.desc: Emeline’s island-hopping escapade in the Philippines unfolds at the Caramoan islands, where she discovers a world of adrenaline-fueled adventures.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Heather explores Požarevac and visits the ancient town of Viminacium. Later visits National Museum, concluding with Majdanpek’s Rajkova Pecina Caves.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: In Bihar’s countryside, Alex gets acquainted with the earthy cuisine that fuels farmers to toil in the fields all day.

programme.title: Xplore
programme.desc: Handicrafts, Dokra art and traditional clay mask from the Kumhars are few thing, we did at Bastar, Chattisgarh.

programme.title: Xplore
programme.desc: Elisha discovers the contemporary side of Vienna at some of its trendiest, most lively neighborhoods.

programme.title: Great World Hotel
programme.desc: Atlantis gets you up close and personal with the marine world, but in a very fishaliciously luxurious fashion.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Heather explores Nis, one of the oldest cities in Europe. Here, she visits a concentration camp built in World War II and later enjoys paragliding wrapping up by watching a musical performance.

programme.desc: Renowned food blogger and caterer, Mehnaz Mansud introduces Eisha to some forgotten recipes of Parsi cuisine.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Sheki, the silk route city of Azerbaijan, is all kinds of indulgence, as Sabrina Chakici finds out. She learns about the unique play of colour and glass that is Shabaka, a traditional art form, and sees how beautifully it has been used in the mesmerizing Sheki Summer House. She also pampers herself with a relaxing crude oil spa treatment, and goes on to explore Khinalug Village, a hidden gem.

programme.title: The Savvy Nomad
programme.sub-title: The Savvy Nomad Koh Tao, Thailand
programme.desc: Emeline visits Koh Tao - a small island in the Gulf of Thailand often referred to as a ‘diver’s paradise’.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Sabrina is in Tiberias, known for its natural hot springs, here she visits the Sea of Galilee. In Nezareth she visits the cave of Mary, ending with Via Dolorosa in Jerusalem.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: Travel back in time and explore the monuments and architecture built by the Mughals such as the Taj Mahal and Agra Fort in Agra and Fatehpur Sikri Fort in Fatehpur Sikhri.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: At Maya Ubud Spa Resort, Jamie Srubis tries different spa treatments that induce the same tranquility which Bali does.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: Surrounded by turquoise waters, pristine beaches and lush tropical greenery, Koh Samui offers Elisha the perfect blend of serenity and luxury.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Once an ancient city on the famous Silk Road, Tashkent is a kaleidoscope of mesmerizing art, delectable food, sweeping gardens and eye-catching architecture. Renowned traveller, Alex Outhwaite explores the Uzbekistan capital, where she visits iconic sights such as the Barak-Khan Madrassah, learns about the city’s history, and tries out some local delicacies such as sheesh kebab, horse milk and horse meat.

programme.title: Bucket List
programme.desc: Daniel Coll & Paddy Doherty try bungee jumping at Kobae Bridge. They also explore nature and mythology at Takachiho.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: Hong Kong the estuary where the east meets the west. The city is facinating in every facet of culture, be it cuisine, music,dance or simply lifestyle.

programme.title: Xplore Jordan
programme.desc: A trip to Jordan leads Shaunak to the ruined city of Jerash, which was buried in sand for hundreds of years until a German explorer rediscovered it. From theatres and temples to the hippodrome, which was once the setting for chariot races and gladiator fights, the ruins of the city offer him a glimpse into the majesty of Roman architecture.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Bulgaria's capital Sofia is one of Europe's most compact and walkable city, with small gardens, parks and cafes sprawled all over.

programme.title: Off The Grid
programme.desc: From understanding stories behind the murals and civil war, enjoying traditional Irish stew to visiting the Peace bridge, Ness County, Benone Strand and Marble Arch Global Geopark, watch Alex enjoy his Londonderry trip.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex Drobin gets to know the vibe of Toronto at significant spots such as Saint Lauren’s Market and Sir Henry’s Garage for Classic Cars.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Azerbaijan has many natural wonders, which could amaze a traveler. From physical mud volcanoes to striking rock formations, Sabrina Chakici witnesses a few of them at Gobustan National Park. Following this, she reaches the city of Ganja, where she uncovers the history at spectacular places such as Heyder Aliyev Park, Mausoleum Imamzadeh, Juma Mosque and Mausoleum Nizami.

programme.title: The Savvy Nomad
programme.desc: At an elephant conservation centre in Lampang, Emeline experiences the immense love that the Thai people feel for their national animal.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Sabrina in Jerusalem, takes a guided tour of City of David and visits Western Wall, learning about its significance. Ending with Cardo and Mahane Yehuda Markets.

programme.title: Bliss
programme.desc: Along with basking in the idyllic beach scenes of Bulgaria, Kirsty admires the art in Helena resort, and experiences the local traditions and cuisine.

programme.title: Best From The Rest
programme.sub-title: Best From The Rest Hong Kong Top Sees
programme.desc: While Hong Kong may be a haven for social media brags, a closer look at some of its most iconic attractions exposes its authentic richness.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: With its rich folk traditions of dance and music, this archipelago is a culmination of fun and frolic to the hilt. With its rich biodiversity.

programme.title: Bucket List
programme.desc: Daniel Coll & Paddy Doherty try some adventurous activities at Mount Aso, the caldera that is home to Japan’s most active volcano.

programme.title: Xplore Jordan
programme.desc: During Shaunak’s visit to Amman, one of the world’s oldest cities, he finds himself taken back to a different stage of the city’s history at every turn: from the Greco-Roman roots in the Roman amphitheater to the iconic Grand Husseini Mosque of the powerful Hashemite dynasty and Qusayr Amra, a desert castle, which served as the fortress of the Ummayad Caliphs.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Valencia, the birthplace of paella, has got the bones of a metropolis and the heart of a quaint seaside town. Alex Outhwaite experiences this when she visits the strikingly futuristic City Of Arts And Sciences and then tries the local specialty, Pinxtos at a Tapas Bar and enjoys Valencia’s laidback charms at El Cabanyal Beach. She also encounters the city’s strong Catholic roots at Cathedral de Valencia, the alleged home of the Holy Grail used by Christ at the Last Supper.

programme.title: Off The Grid
programme.desc: Alex explores Cork, enjoys Irish meal and music, visits Fota Wildlife Park, Blarney castle. He then explores Cork butter museum, ending with a visit to Cork City Gaol-a former prison but now a museum.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From the famous deer meat pie of Aux Ancies Canadiens to the art on Rue Du Tresor, Alex Drobin soaks in the vibes of sophisticated yet playful Quebec.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Sabrina Chakici marvels at the architectural wonders of Baku, the capital of Azerbaijan, while also checking out souvenirs at the local bazaar.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Sabrina visits Ein Gedi oasis and Masada plateau on her way to Eilat, where she visits the beaches, Red Canyon and Wadi Shani.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex Drobin experiences the North American energy and European charm of Montreal as he explores all that the city has to offer.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: With a rich history dating back to over 1000 years, Oslo’s iconic monuments reveal its evolution through the ages to visitors.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: While the vast majority of Rajasthan’s inhabitants have always been staunch vegetarians, there has been tremendous popularity of the meat dishes in the state’s culinary culture. This came mainly from the Rajput rulers, which is why Alex meets Ajayraj Singh who comes from a royal lineage. He introduces her to the history of the valiant rulers of Mewar and some of the secret meat preparations of the kingdom

programme.title: Timeless Tamil Nadu
programme.desc: In the last leg of her journey in Tamil Nadu, Alex visits one of India’s southernmost towns, Rameshwaram. Here, she is treated to stunning views of azure waters and coral islands that stretch all the way to Sri Lanka. What she doesn't expect to find, however, are the ruins of a ghost town sitting on a beach.

programme.title: Xplore India
programme.desc: Aradhana embarks on an adventure to find out the hidden wonders of a land steeped in spirituality and untamed wilderness: Chhattisgarh!

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Visit Belgrade, with Heather as she explores the city, its architecture and learns about its past by visiting historical monuments.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: During Elisha’s visit to Oslo, she finds herself in awe of the extraordinary well-designed ships used by the legendary Vikings of Norway.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: Where many ancient Indian civilizations flourished in the most resource-filled areas, the people of Rajasthan created a powerful, complex, layered and regal culture and cuisine in the largely arid land that they called home. Alex discovers this when she meets Anjali Singh, a member of the royal family of Jaipur, who shows her how to make two prized recipes from her family’s legacy - Papad Ki Chutney and Safed Murg.

programme.title: The Savvy Nomad Phillippines
programme.desc: Emeline explores Manila's historic Intramuros, where she gawks at colonial relics before diving into the city's vibrant chaos aboard a jeepney.

programme.title: Xplore India
programme.desc: As Aradhana’s journey in Chhattisgarh continues, she is stunned to discover the wealth of natural beauty that the state has to offer.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Novi Sad, the financial and industrial hub of Serbia.Starting with canoeing on Danube and later exploring the city and its landmarks.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: Bihar’s rural heartland has a lot to offer the intrepid traveller. Alex meets Rajesh Ramjan, a politician, a cultural conservationist and a foodie, who shows her the beauty of Bihar’s countryside and introduces her to some traditional Bihari dishes. From dahi-chura, the Bihari breakfast of champions to flavourful preparations of fresh local vegetables simmering on a traditional stove, Alex gets an understanding of the nourishing cuisine that fuels farmers to toil in the fields all day.

programme.title: Xplore India
programme.desc: On the last leg of her journey in Chhattisgarh, Aradhana uncovers the hidden world of the state’s artistic legacy.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.desc: Catch the beauty of Prague through its fascinating history, its classic mix of Gothic and Renaissance architecture and more.

programme.desc: Known as Druk Yul or the “Land of the Thunder Dragon”, Bhutan is the country of mountains, monasteries and mystery. Radhika Bangia finds this to be true when she visits Punakha, the former capital of Bhutan, where she marvels at the architecture of Punakha Dzong and gets to know the story behind the two rivers, Mo Chhu and Po Chhu. She also explores the sacred temples and Mebar Tsho lake of Bumthang, the religious heart of Bhutan, and gets to know the local legends.

programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Like Rome, Plovdiv straddles seven hills; but as Europe’s oldest continuously inhabited city, it’s far more ancient. As Catriona takes a tour of the city to see its iconic attractions such as a Roman Amphitheatre, which was once the setting for gladiator fights, and the striking Baroque Saints Constantine and Helena Church, she begins to see how seamlessly modern life blends with the age-old ruins. Along the way, she realizes why Plovdiv is called the ‘Eternal City’ of Bulgaria.

programme.title: Les Estaminets
programme.desc: Pousser la porte d'un estaminet, c'est s'offrir un voyage nostalgique dans le passé.

programme.title: SLAY
programme.desc: Follow investigative filmmaker Rebecca Cappelli as she travels around the world to uncover the dark side of the fashion industry.

programme.title: A Space in Time
programme.desc: Witness the extraordinary spirit of a family who choose to see their sons' challenges as superpowers, and celebrate the beauty of life, redefined.

programme.title: MILKED
programme.desc: This gripping investigation unmasks how the million-dollar cash-cow industry has been milked dry.

programme.title: Amber Tamm
programme.desc: Amber Tamm is a young woman from Brooklyn who is a horticulturist, floral designer, community advocate, and farmer.

programme.title: Dance for Change
programme.desc: An inspiring celebration of the Dance Crews of Dzaleka Refugee camp, who are transforming their community through creative expression.

programme.title: Lowest to Highest: One Foot in Front of the Other
programme.desc: A story of overcoming personal hurdles, resilience, connection, and how adversity can strengthen communities and individuals.

programme.title: Connected Off the Grid
programme.desc: Connected Off the Grid follows the stories of three unique off-grid lifestyles from the ocean, the desert, and the redwood forest to inspire us to stay connected to the earth and their communities.

programme.title: Against the Grain
programme.desc: The story of one of the most extensive permaculture experiments in the UK.

programme.title: Particle Physics
programme.desc: At only 21, Frank Wilczek, a graduate student at Princeton University, defined the properties of color gluons, which hold atomic nuclei together while working with David Gross. He is known, among other things, for the discovery of asymptotic freedom, the development of quantum chromodynamics, the invention of axions, and the discovery and exploitation of new forms of quantum statistics (anyons).

programme.title: Consciousness of Culture
programme.desc: Bruce Parry highlights the moments of his life that formed his career and life passion to champion indigenous cultures around the world through exploration and film. Bruce is interested in altered states of mind, and the creation of myths, why do we think what we think? And what life phenomena and environments sculpt these ideas?

programme.title: The Nature of All Things
programme.desc: What is it that makes things what they are? Where does it come from? For Professor Seyyed Hossein Nasr, the quest for knowledge, specifically knowledge which enables man to understand the true nature of things and which furthermore, liberates and delivers him from the fetters and limitations of earthly existence, has been (and continues to be) the central concern and determinant of how we understand and live our lives.

programme.title: Cosmopsychism
programme.desc: This episode of Curious Minds with Yujin Nagasawa discusses the unique idea of cosmopsychism; an idea which argues our personal conscious experiences are segments of a larger consciousness that belongs to the cosmos. Yujin Nagasawas life work lies in the mystery of consciousness, existence and the philosophy of religion. This controversial conversation with Deepak Chopra uncovers new theories and explanations for consciousness, as they strive to uncover truth.

programme.title: Consciousness & Healing
programme.desc: Dr. Shamini Jain conducts clinical research in the areas of psychoneuroimmunology and integrative medicine, receiving several awards from scientific organizations for her work. Shes been continuously interested in the process of healing and strived to uncover many of the doubts she had in undergraduate school when she discovered the field psychoneuroimmunology; the integrated relationship between mind, brain and body.

programme.title: Book Of Knowledge
programme.desc: Traveling the world 30+ years, David Verdesi finds himself back in Rome. Urged to uncover the reason behind the instinctive human desire for spiritual yearning, Verdesi starts retracing the intersection of science and theology within the city his family has called home since the early 1400s. Vibrating from the walls of the citys sacred spaces, he discovers findings of oral history, predating the beginning of humanity by thousands of years. Documents and rare rituals that upend conventional ideas of the behaviors of the mind in ancient times. Narratives heavily influenced by civilizations and cultures beyond Rome, continue to influence the moral compass of life today, Verdesi realizes he is back on the road. Book of Knowledge The Quest for Meaning rediscovers and preserves the knowledge of our human potential and natures wisdom. This movie addresses questions around the intersection of science, spirituality, and anthropology. Join David Verdesi to the roots of the Book of Knowledge.

programme.title: Particle Physics
programme.desc: At only 21, Frank Wilczek, a graduate student at Princeton University, defined the properties of color gluons, which hold atomic nuclei together while working with David Gross. He is known, among other things, for the discovery of asymptotic freedom, the development of quantum chromodynamics, the invention of axions, and the discovery and exploitation of new forms of quantum statistics (anyons).

programme.title: Consciousness of Culture
programme.desc: Bruce Parry highlights the moments of his life that formed his career and life passion to champion indigenous cultures around the world through exploration and film. Bruce is interested in altered states of mind, and the creation of myths, why do we think what we think? And what life phenomena and environments sculpt these ideas?

programme.title: The Nature of All Things
programme.desc: What is it that makes things what they are? Where does it come from? For Professor Seyyed Hossein Nasr, the quest for knowledge, specifically knowledge which enables man to understand the true nature of things and which furthermore, liberates and delivers him from the fetters and limitations of earthly existence, has been (and continues to be) the central concern and determinant of how we understand and live our lives.

programme.title: Astrology Zone
programme.desc: Susan Miller is the founder and astrologist for AstrologyZone.com. The trusted, twenty-year-old platform provides the latest in intelligent astrological trends. This discussion with Deepak Chopra breaks down many popular misconceptions about astrological predictions, the various disciplines of study, ego, and consciousness in relation to astrology. Miller also outlines the golden rule to understanding and getting the most out of astrological predications: astrology shows the condition, but not the outcomethat outcome is completely up to you. I can give you questions to ask to help you decide, but in the end, you write the script of your life.

programme.title: Modern Metaphysical Idealism
programme.desc: Dr. Bernardo Kastrup, philosopher and author has been leading the modern renaissance of metaphysical idealism. His theoretical reflections on the mind explain consciousness as the only element of life that allows us to understand reality, and everything we know about reality is subject to our experiences. This idea is often refuted by the thought that the brain is the source, not images and experiences provided by consciousness.

programme.title: Nature, Space and Matter
programme.desc: Our understanding of the nature of time, space and matter have changedeven what we mean by these termsin just the last few decades. Our interpretations continue to change and more theories form as we learn and study these topics. Dr. Fred Alan Wolf explains the origins of many of these foundational theories that have formed at different times and why they came about. Before quantum physics, people knew that human beings were conscious. We knew that animals were conscious. But now, with new revelations in quantum physics we begin to see a new role for consciousness.

programme.title: Creation Is Constant
programme.desc: Duane Elgin wants to show that the universe is a living field of existence, and that we are always in communion with a field of aliveness whether we are conscious of it or not. Throughout this lecture, Elgin explains his belief that in a living system, each part is integral to the whole, so each entity is intimately connected to the entire universe. To him, creation is not something that happened with the Big Bang. Instead, creation is consistently forming around us from a deeper abyss. The universes existence is brought moment by moment through this continuous, moving process.

programme.title: Consciousness & Healing
programme.desc: Dr. Shamini Jain conducts clinical research in the areas of psychoneuroimmunology and integrative medicine, receiving several awards from scientific organizations for her work. Shes been continuously interested in the process of healing and strived to uncover many of the doubts she had in undergraduate school when she discovered the field psychoneuroimmunology; the integrated relationship between mind, brain and body.

programme.title: Borderline Consciousness
programme.desc: Berit Brogaard was the first researcher to show that consciousness comes in degrees and that there can be borderline cases of consciousness. Her areas of research include perception, consciousness, emotions, philosophical psychology, semantics and philosophical logic. She also researches synesthesia, savant syndrome, blind sight, and perceptual reports.

programme.title: The Knobe Effect
programme.desc: We have experiences, creativity, understanding, thoughts, feelings and desires but do we know how these occurrences happen? Joshua Knobes research has been concerned with the impact of people's moral judgments on their intuitions about this question. He believes strongly that people's intuitions about whether a particular entity is conscious, essentially depends on whether the entity has the right sort of body. Therefore, he believes embodiment shapes our view of what is reality.

programme.title: Quantifying Beauty
programme.desc: How do we scientifically quantify beauty in people and in nature? How does the world embody beautiful ideas? Dr. Frank Wilczeks conversation with Deepak Chopra discusses his book, A Beautiful Question which tests the common misconceptions about how we form our opinions on success, beauty and our imaginations.

programme.title: Connections Between Science & Philosophy
programme.desc: Dr. Menas Kafatos is internationally known for bridging the disciplines, writing and lecturing extensively on the convergence between science and philosophy, as there is an inherent need for a dialogue between science, spirituality, and religion. His book, Creating Cosmos with Deepak Chopra discusses the melding of topics like inflation, environmental issues, natural hazards, quantum theory and consciousness.

programme.title: Iranian Space Pioneer
programme.desc: Iranian electrical engineer, entrepreneur and former astronaut, Anousheh Ansari was the first female private space explorer to fly to the International Space Station. She explains the challenging journey and the determination needed to reach space after immigrating to the U.S. from Iran. She also became the first person to publish a blog from space. The content invited readers to share her experience with the world. She described the Russian Soyuz capsule during liftoff, talked about the sights and smells of the space station, and explained the intricacies of everyday activities.

programme.title: Philosopher of the Mind
programme.desc: Dr. Paavo Pylkknens eclectic approach combines new physics-based insights with those of analytical philosophy, phenomenology, cognitive science and neuroscience. He proposes a view in which the mechanistic framework of classical physics and neuroscience is complemented by a more holistic approach. Pylkknen is particularly known for his work on mind-body-studies, building on David Bohm's interpretation of quantum mechanics.

programme.title: Evolution of Ideas
programme.desc: Everything we can know about our lives and the universe is a subjective experience, either formed by one's own perceptions or the reports of others. Dr. Bernardo Kastrup explains how we commonly associate this understanding about the universe as something separate of our consciousness, however he strongly believes otherwise; Kastrup believes the evolution of ideas we perceive about the universe are actually originating within us.

programme.title: Book Of Knowledge
programme.desc: Traveling the world 30+ years, David Verdesi finds himself back in Rome. Urged to uncover the reason behind the instinctive human desire for spiritual yearning, Verdesi starts retracing the intersection of science and theology within the city his family has called home since the early 1400s. Documents and rare rituals that upend conventional ideas of the behaviors of the mind in ancient times. Narratives heavily influenced by civilizations and cultures beyond Rome, continue to influence the moral compass of life today, Verdesi realizes he is back on the road. Book of Knowledge The Quest for Meaning rediscovers and preserves the knowledge of our human potential and natures wisdom. This movie addresses questions around the intersection of science, spirituality, and anthropology. Join David Verdesi to the roots of the Book of Knowledge.

programme.title: Evolution of Ideas
programme.desc: Everything we can know about our lives and the universe is a subjective experience, either formed by one's own perceptions or the reports of others. Dr. Bernardo Kastrup explains how we commonly associate this understanding about the universe as something separate of our consciousness, however he strongly believes otherwise; Kastrup believes the evolution of ideas we perceive about the universe are actually originating within us.

programme.title: Book Of Knowledge
programme.desc: Traveling the world 30+ years, David Verdesi finds himself back in Rome. Urged to uncover the reason behind the instinctive human desire for spiritual yearning, Verdesi starts retracing the intersection of science and theology within the city his family has called home since the early 1400s. Vibrating from the walls of the citys sacred spaces, he discovers findings of oral history, predating the beginning of humanity by thousands of years. Documents and rare rituals that upend conventional ideas of the behaviors of the mind in ancient times. Narratives heavily influenced by civilizations and cultures beyond Rome, continue to influence the moral compass of life today, Verdesi realizes he is back on the road. Book of Knowledge The Quest for Meaning rediscovers and preserves the knowledge of our human potential and natures wisdom. This movie addresses questions around the intersection of science, spirituality, and anthropology. Join David Verdesi to the roots of the Book of Knowledge.

programme.title: Particle Physics
programme.desc: At only 21, Frank Wilczek, a graduate student at Princeton University, defined the properties of color gluons, which hold atomic nuclei together while working with David Gross. He is known, among other things, for the discovery of asymptotic freedom, the development of quantum chromodynamics, the invention of axions, and the discovery and exploitation of new forms of quantum statistics (anyons).

programme.title: Astrology Zone
programme.desc: Susan Miller is the founder and astrologist for AstrologyZone.com. The trusted, twenty-year-old platform provides the latest in intelligent astrological trends. This discussion with Deepak Chopra breaks down many popular misconceptions about astrological predictions, the various disciplines of study, ego, and consciousness in relation to astrology. Miller also outlines the golden rule to understanding and getting the most out of astrological predications: astrology shows the condition, but not the outcomethat outcome is completely up to you. I can give you questions to ask to help you decide, but in the end, you write the script of your life.

programme.title: The Nature of All Things
programme.desc: What is it that makes things what they are? Where does it come from? For Professor Seyyed Hossein Nasr, the quest for knowledge, specifically knowledge which enables man to understand the true nature of things and which furthermore, liberates and delivers him from the fetters and limitations of earthly existence, has been (and continues to be) the central concern and determinant of how we understand and live our lives.

programme.title: SandCastle: The Secret Life of Potter Wasps
programme.desc: Join a courageous potter wasp on an enthralling journey!

programme.title: Apayauq
programme.desc: Follow the story of Apayauq Reitan, the first openly transgender musher to compete in the Iditarod.

programme.title: Ocean's Rose
programme.desc: Ocean’s Rose tells the story of the world’s first shark whisperer.

programme.title: Dawn Days
programme.desc: Surfer/photographer Mike Guest capture the dawn every morning of the pandemic in this beautiful project.

programme.title: YARROW: The Virtues of Monochrome
programme.desc: Fine art photographer David Yarrow steps ashore the mind-blowing wildlife scenes of South Georgia.

programme.title: Water Has a Memory
programme.desc: Join Casey Camp-Horinek, Matriarch and Environmental Ambassador for the Ponca Nation, as she leads us through the occupied territory of Ponca City, Oklahoma. Through ancestral teachings, she is protecting the Water, Mother Earth, and Father Sky through the Rights of Nature and climate justice, standing up against industry giants for Indigenous rights and future generations.

programme.title: KUNAMA
programme.desc: We pay respects to the traditional owners of the Ngarigo Country, elders past, present and future.

programme.title: The Leap Beneath
programme.desc: A lighthearted film about the epic quest of a globular springtail navigating through the soil to find food.

programme.title: 47 Million Bees
programme.desc: Biologist Paola Vecino works to preserve the native Iberian honey bee in Spain.

programme.title: Empathy in Africa
programme.desc: “Empathy in Africa'' follows three women who personify resilience in the face of obstacles.

programme.title: Before The Oil
programme.desc: Teaching about the energy transition from before the oil was found until the present day, respect for nature and choices we have to make.

programme.title: The Conservation Kid
programme.desc: At the age of seven, “The Conservation Kid,” began leading cleanups in the Tennessee River in his hometown.

programme.title: Caddisfly
programme.desc: Dr. John Morse reflects on how his passion for teaching has influenced a generation of scientists.

programme.title: Watershed Warriors
programme.desc: Meet two river activists from Friends of the Fox River (FOTFR), Gary Swick and Jenni Kempf.

programme.title: Heart of the Osa
programme.desc: A story about how different NGOs are making a difference fighting for wildlife in the Osa Peninsula.

programme.title: Interbeing
programme.desc: Interbeing is a multi-media project which celebrates traditional music and the beauty of Romania

programme.title: Selati Game Reserve
programme.desc: Spotlighting cheetah conservation at Selati Wilderness Foundation.

programme.title: Selati Game Reserve
programme.desc: We spotlight the critical role of African vultures, five of which are now Critically Endangered.

programme.title: Kalu: Growing Up Wild
programme.desc: A documentary about the challenges a baby spider monkey faces growing up in the jungle of the Osa Peninsula

programme.title: Finding Solo
programme.desc: Malaysian filmmaker Myles Storey searches for Solo, a white-handed gibbon believed to be the last of his kind

programme.title: 41 Degrees North
programme.desc: Immerse yourself in the tranquil rhythms of Shenyang with Barry Wilkinson's 41 Degrees North.

programme.title: ReCoral
programme.desc: A proof-of-concept coral restoration trial in partnership with Penghu Marine Biology Research Center.

programme.title: Muga
programme.desc: A river's journey to reclaim life.

programme.title: Selati Game Reserve
programme.desc: Discover how Selati manages its elephant population and supports vital conservation efforts.

programme.title: Selati Game Reserve
programme.desc: Rhinos are iconic and endangered. If we can’t protect them, what can we protect?

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Centuries of pollution despoiled New York Harbor, turning it into a virtual dead zone. But now, the most ambitious restoration project in any city on Earth aims to bring it back to life.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: In southeast Florida, conservationists are out at dawn every morning, surveying and protecting the nests of three turtle species to keep them safe from human traffic.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Crystal River has long been a safe haven for the Florida manatee, but when an invasive algae wiped out the eelgrass that manatees need for food, the community rallied to restore the river and save the animals that call it home.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Coral reefs around the world are threatened by rising ocean temperatures, but hope is growing off the coast of Hawaii. There, researchers at the Coral Resilience Lab selectively breed corals to withstand ever-increasing amounts of heat stress.

programme.title: Cornwall's Climate Stories
programme.desc: Highlighting the impacts of climate change already being felt around the county of Cornwall - and meeting the inspiring people, communities and researchers working to tackle it.

programme.title: Cornwall's Climate Stories
programme.desc: A passionate ultra-runner travels around the Cornish coast path, witnessing the ever-increasing impacts of coastal erosion.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: All around the world, seabirds provide a critical link between land and sea. On Hawai’i, ecologists are working to protect two vital shearwater species that helped life first take hold across these islands.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: On Panama’s remote Pearl Islands, marine biologist Callie Veelenturf is using her research on endangered sea turtles to help the country enshrine the rights of nature into law—and help the communities living there protect their own environment and livelihood.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: A decade after a momentous dam removal, salmon are helping revive a lost U.S. ecosystem.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Farmers grow coffee to save a rainforest that’s crucial to local communities and wildlife.

programme.title: Cornwall's Climate Stories
programme.desc: This episode delves deep into issues around climate change and Cornwall’s fresh water – looking at everything from heavier rain leading to more sewage overflows, flooding and loss of topsoil, to how warmer river water is affecting fish, and the surprising carbon footprint of the water we use every day.

programme.title: Cornwall's Climate Stories
programme.desc: We’ve all heard headlines heralding the meat and dairy industry as the villains of the climate crisis, but is this really true?

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Australian scientists enlist conservation dogs to save threatened native species.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: In Brazil’s Atlantic Forest, Mauricio Ruiz has turned his love for nature into action by working with the community to reforest a critical stretch of the nation’s most endangered forest, and by using drones to help him reach his goal of planting 15 million trees.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Fernanda Abra leads a crucial conservation initiative along the roadways of Brazil, where vehicles annually kill about 475 million vertebrates - more than double the country’s human population.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Inventor Boyan Slat is on a mission to rid oceans of plastic.

programme.title: Cornwall's Climate Stories
programme.desc: Today's industrialised food system causes a huge amount of our carbon emissions - and we waste one-third of all the food produced.

programme.title: Groundwork
programme.sub-title: Groundwork Ep1 - “Cotton”, A Family Journey into Regenerative Cotton
programme.desc: Shifting from conventional farming practices to regenerative agriculture takes courage.

programme.title: Mutinda
programme.desc: The transformational story of Kenya’s once most wanted Poacher, Mutinda Ndivo.

programme.title: The North Face Presents: Groundwork
programme.sub-title: Groundwork: Building Community Through Regenerative Rubber
programme.desc: Though Thailand leads in rubber exports, Chorthip, a rare female rubber farmer, is diversifying her crops and using organic methods to benefit her land and community.

programme.title: Goat Surf Club
programme.sub-title: Goat Surf Club in Senegal ep.01
programme.desc: In Senegal, Roo, Ben and Thomas meet with L'Homme Plastique, aka "The Plastic Man", and learn about plastic pollution.

programme.title: Goat Surf Club
programme.sub-title: Goat Surf Club in Senegal ep.02
programme.desc: Get ready to shred with the Goat Surf Club! This episode scores them a lesson from the Surfrider foundation on keeping Senegalese beaches plastic-free. Then, it's all about dodging sea urchins and jellyfish for epic waves.

programme.title: Kofi and Lartey
programme.desc: The story of a man who escaped one of the most toxic places in the world, and his fight to try and empower two young boys to do the same.

programme.title: Goat Surf Club
programme.sub-title: Goat Surf Club in Senegal ep.03
programme.desc: The Goat Surf Club visits the vibrant town of St. Louis for a taste of Senegalese culture – food, dance, and music! They'll also shred some sand dunes on a desert sandboard before hitting the waves with a local surf coach.

programme.title: Goat Surf Club
programme.sub-title: Goat Surf Club in Senegal ep.04
programme.desc: The Goat Surf Club's Senegalese finale hits a bumpy wave! Planned eco-heroes spotlights get sidelined by protests, but the crew scores big time with a kind Senegalese family who keeps them safe.

programme.title: Follow the Wind
programme.desc: Jerrie van de Kop follows the wind as he kiteboards from warm waters to snowy peaks.

programme.title: Across the Ice
programme.desc: After 45+ expeditions throughout over 150+ countries, one of the world's leading adventurers and visionaries of conservation, Swedish explorer Johan Ernst Nilson dawns upon his biggest adventure yet. Follow his journey across the planet from the North Pole to the South Pole in Pole2Pole. The 525 day mostly carbon-neutral trek covers 20,000 miles by means of skis, dogsled, boat, bike and kite. Pole2Pole is not only one of the most difficult adventures hes ever attempted, but also one of his most philanthropic adventures. Johan visits charities along the way to share their stories and raise global awareness.

programme.title: The Wake
programme.desc: A Scottish fisherman struggles to keep his craft alive in the face of ecological destruction from industrial-scale trawling.

programme.title: Grounded
programme.desc: In the rugged mountains of western Montana, a team of researchers makes a chilling discovery: the golden eagles they study are dying from a mysterious illness.

programme.title: Seb's Surf Therapy
programme.desc: Seb is a charismatic 12-year-old with ADHD and autism who embarks on a six-week surf therapy course.

programme.title: Chasing Bliss
programme.desc: Water is life, and the great conductor that connects us all. The oceans cover over 70% of our planet and hold almost 97% of the earth's water. True bliss in constant movement giving life around the globe. This is a story of an eclectic mix of people across Hawaii, Ecuador and the USA, who draw energy from this life force and channel it positively into their everyday lives. Join Paulino in understanding what drives Surfer Laid Hamilton, or filmmaker Robert Greaves, Big-Wave World Champion Paige Alms and Windsurfing World Record Holder Sarah Hauser. Coffee Farmer Anti Clancy, Dr Greg Mitchell, Research Scientist & Algae Visionary at Scripps.

programme.title: I Am Capable
programme.desc: After experiencing injustice when hiking in the English countryside, Amira Patel decided she wanted to help create a safe space for Muslim women outdoors.

programme.title: The Last Vaquita
programme.desc: Caught up in a tale of greed and corruption, two brothers who collaborate as wildlife artists try to bring the Vaquita Porpoise back from the brink of extinction.

programme.title: JOY
programme.desc: How would you feel if you had to combine the maternity of your two children with the protection of some of the last mountain gorillas still living in freedom on our planet?

programme.title: Live to Sea
programme.desc: A saga that follows Freddie Meadows on his tireless quest along the rugged edges of Sweden, in search of the region’s greatest waves; one of the final frontiers within surfing.

programme.title: Return of the Rhino
programme.desc: In 2021, after a thirty year absence, black rhino were reintroduced to the wilds of Gonarezhou National Park in Zimbabwe. This is a story about hope, truth and the claiming of a much needed foothold for the critically endangered black rhino species.

programme.title: Hellfire Heroes

programme.title: Storm Hunters
programme.desc: A mysterious fever overwhelms Mark as he struggles to fulfil a childhood dream. He must use every ounce of inner strength to journey to the mouth of hell, and make it out alive.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.sub-title: Angry Planet | By Camel to Timbuktu
programme.desc: It is time for “Angry Planet” to do a serious desert episode. What better place to do it than this fabled city in the middle of the mighty Sahara Desert.This almost mythical place really does exist. And it really is still possible to travel there by camel caravan, riding the infamous “Ships of the Desert”. Extreme heat, dehydration, possible sand storms, frigid night temperatures and desert predators are our potential companions for the trip.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.sub-title: Angry Planet | Java - Land of Fire
programme.desc: Geoorge explores Java at the heart of the Ring of Fire.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Possibly the home of the deadly Marberg Ebola virus, Kitum Cave is high on Mt.Elgon straddling the Kenya-Uganda border. The deep, waterfall-shrouded cave has been carved out over millennia by elephants in search of salt. When host george kourounis gets bitten by one of the bats…well, you’ll have to see the show to find out what happens.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Costa Rica is a tiny country jammed with diversity and adventure. George explores the volcanoes of Costa Rica with an international team of scientists, rafts down whitewater rivers in full flood, canyoneers down raging waterfalls, and handles some of deadliest snakes this tropical country has to offer.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: George links up with two experienced polar adventurers to explore the frigid Canadian arctic in the depths of winter. The team first uses skis to cross the fractured sea ice of Frobisher Bay, then dog teams to head north through the spectacular Auyuittuq Mountains.

programme.title: Angry Planet

programme.desc: Alaskan bush pilots risk everything to save hunters struggling to stay alive in a remote area of Kodiak.

programme.title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots
programme.sub-title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots | Hanging by a Thread
programme.desc: Tanalian Aviation bush pilots Joel and Josiah earn their keep slinging a stranded snow machine.

programme.title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots
programme.sub-title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots | Ice Climbers
programme.desc: Tanalian Aviation battles winter weather to ferry ice climbers onto the Knik Glacier.

programme.title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots
programme.sub-title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots | Risky Business
programme.desc: Pilot Peter Rosendahl combats gail force wind while taxiing a group of hunters to a floating lodge.

programme.title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots
programme.sub-title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots | Surf's Up
programme.desc: Rough seas have a couple of surfers turning to Island Air for an adventure of a life time.

programme.title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots
programme.sub-title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots | On Thin Ice
programme.desc: On a rare day off, Josiah and his friends white water raft where few have gone before - on top of the massive Knik Glacier.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown

programme.desc: Do you ever wish you could see all the wackiest weather from around the world, without moving a muscle or lifting a finger? Your wish is our command here on a brand new episode of Extreme Weather Countdown!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown
programme.desc: We have a brand new lineup bursting at the seams with some of the craziest climate events caught on camera.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown
programme.desc: From the intense to the unusual to the just plain wacky, Extreme Weather Countdown is home to everything the elements have to offer, and it's time for an all-new episode. We'll work our way through snow, wind, fog, lightning, and more, so fasten your seatbelts and let's get started.

programme.title: Life on the Edge - S01:E05 - From Inquisitive Bears to Caribou
programme.desc: Ein neugieriger Bär stört Charlie Summervilles Baupläne; Pat Harvie zeigt seiner Frau Connie, wie man in den Aleuten Lachs angelt, und Merrick Jackinsky geht auf die Suche nach Karibus.

programme.title: Life on the Edge - S01:E06 - Missing
programme.desc: Charlie Summerville verschwindet, während er versucht, Baumaterialien mit dem Boot zu transportieren. In der Zwischenzeit müssen Pat und Connie Harvie mit einer Flut von Vulkanexperten umgehen, die auf der isoliertesten Ranch Amerikas auftauchen.

programme.title: Wardens Case Files - S01:E04 - Badge Bust and Beluga Whale
programme.desc: In 'Badge Bust' führt das Übervertrauen eines Abzeichens dazu, dass ein anderes Abzeichen in Schwierigkeiten gerät. Inzwischen erzählt 'Beluga Whale' die Geschichte eines Meereslebewesens, das weit weg von zu Hause ist und ein echtes Abenteuer erlebt.

programme.title: Wardens Case Files - S01:E05 - North Coast and Seat Belt
programme.desc: Wildhüter nehmen sich einem der größten Fälle in der 146-jährigen Geschichte der Wildlife-Abteilung von Ohio an. Sie müssen herausfinden, was passiert ist, als eine Gruppe von Menschen das Gesetz brach. In 'Sicherheitsgurt' führt eine routinemäßige Verkehrskontrolle zu einer überraschenden Entdeckung.

programme.title: Wardens Case Files - S01:E06 - Lobster Poaching and Black Walnut
programme.desc: In 'Hummer-Wilderei' hat ein Wilderer einen schlechten Tag, weil er in das falsche Boot springt. Währenddessen erzählt 'Schwarze Walnuss' eine Geschichte über eine überraschende Spezies, die die Aufmerksamkeit eines Wildhüters auf sich zieht. Wildhüter erinnern sich an unvergessliche Fälle und Erlebnisse aus ihrem Beruf.

programme.title: Wildlife Heroes - S01:E04 - Aggressive Measures
programme.desc: Ivan reist nach Mosambik, um einem Anti-Wilderei-Team beizutreten, das sich darauf konzentriert, Wilderer zu fangen und gefährdete Tiere zu schützen.

programme.title: Wildlife Heroes - S01:E05 - Extreme Conditions
programme.desc: Naturschützer fangen und studieren Weißwedelhirsch im kalten Winter von South Dakota.

programme.title: Wildlife Heroes - S01:E06 - Capturing the Wild
programme.desc: Naturschützer setzen raffinierte Taktiken ein, um Kudu, Impala und Giraffen zu fangen.

programme.title: Wardens - S05:E05 - Wardens Instinct
programme.desc: Es gibt oft eine feine Linie zwischen falschen Informationen und der Wahrheit. Veteranen der Wardens von Region 5 nutzen ihren sechsten Sinn, um Wildtierverletzer zur Rechenschaft zu ziehen.

programme.title: Wardens - S05:E06 - Fourth Dimension
programme.desc: Der Vierte Juli am Flathead Lake in Montana ist der Ort, an dem die Party stattfindet. Aber bei der Hitze kochen die Gemüter hoch und der ungehinderte Fluss von Alkohol macht die Situation gefährlich.

programme.title: Wardens - S05:E07 - Behind The Shed
programme.desc: Wenn die Wildgebiete für die Jagd auf Geweihabwurf geöffnet werden, sind alle Hände an Deck für die Wildhüter in Montana. Berichte über Bären, Berglöwen und verlorene Jäger halten die Wildhüter auf Trab.

programme.title: Life on the Edge - S01:E04 - Building a Wilderness Cabin
programme.desc: In Volcano Bay beginnen die Nerven von Charlie Summerville und seinem Team zu fransen, während sie mit dem Bau der Wildnishütte beginnen.

programme.title: Life on the Edge - S01:E05 - From Inquisitive Bears to Caribou
programme.desc: Ein neugieriger Bär stört Charlie Summervilles Baupläne; Pat Harvie zeigt seiner Frau Connie, wie man in den Aleuten Lachs angelt, und Merrick Jackinsky geht auf die Suche nach Karibus.

programme.title: Wardens Case Files - S01:E04 - Badge Bust and Beluga Whale
programme.desc: In 'Badge Bust' führt das Übervertrauen eines Abzeichens dazu, dass ein anderes Abzeichen in Schwierigkeiten gerät. Inzwischen erzählt 'Beluga Whale' die Geschichte eines Meereslebewesens, das weit weg von zu Hause ist und ein echtes Abenteuer erlebt.

programme.title: Wardens Case Files - S01:E05 - North Coast and Seat Belt
programme.desc: Wildhüter nehmen sich einem der größten Fälle in der 146-jährigen Geschichte der Wildlife-Abteilung von Ohio an. Sie müssen herausfinden, was passiert ist, als eine Gruppe von Menschen das Gesetz brach. In 'Sicherheitsgurt' führt eine routinemäßige Verkehrskontrolle zu einer überraschenden Entdeckung.

programme.title: Dropped - S03:E09 - Bull In The Bush
programme.desc: Nach mehr als drei Wochen Wandern durch Elchland schlagen die Brüder Chris und Casey Keefer endlich ein Lager an einem neuen Ort auf und stehen fast sofort einem gewaltigen Bullen gegenüber.

programme.title: Dropped - S03:E10 - End Of The Line
programme.desc: Mit den Chancen gegen sie ist das 80-Meilen-Flussabenteuer fast vorbei, und die Brüder Chris und Casey Keefer haben eine letzte Gelegenheit, einen Alaskamoose nur wenige Meilen von dem Ort zu finden, an dem sie an der Flusskreuzung abgeholt werden.

programme.title: Dropped - S04:E01 - To Be Alive
programme.desc: Chris und Casey Keefer sehen sich Videoeinträge an, um ein glückliches Paar auszuwählen, das mit ihnen nach Alaska kommt. Die Jäger treffen sich zum ersten Mal am Flughafen. Casey und Kaid werden abgesetzt, während Chris und der Rest am Hangar auf Verzögerungen stoßen.

programme.title: Dropped - S04:E02 - Separation Anxiety
programme.desc: Das Wetter in Alaska wird gerade so mild, dass Chris und Kaid ihr Ziel erreichen und etwas zu essen fangen können. Währenddessen haben Casey und R.L. bereits ihr Camp eingerichtet und bereiten sich auf die Elch-Saison vor. Die Brüder müssen sich daran gewöhnen, zum ersten Mal getrennt abgesetzt zu werden.

programme.title: Dropped - S04:E03 - Predators Prowl
programme.desc: Chris und Kaid bereiteten ihre Rafts vor, während Casey und R.L. begannen, nach Schwarzbären und Elchen Ausschau zu halten. Just in diesem Moment bemerkten Chris und Kaid einen Raubtier, das sich ihrem Lagerplatz näherte.

programme.title: Desperate Hours - S01:E02 - Earthquake
programme.desc: Wenn sich die Erde wirklich bewegt, bedeutet das eine Katastrophe epischen Ausmaßes. Desperate Hours befasst sich mit der zerstörerischen Kraft von Erdbeben und betrachtet das Kathmandu-Erdbeben 2015 in Nepal, das Sichuan-Erdbeben 2008 in China, bei dem schätzungsweise 10.000 Kinder in eingestürzten Schulgebäuden eingeschlossen waren, ein Puerto-Mont-Erdbeben 1960 in Chile, das Port-au-Princ-Erdbeben 2010 in Haiti und das Kaschmir-Erdbeben 2005 in Pakistan.

programme.title: Desperate Hours - S01:E03 - Epidemic
programme.desc: Desperate Hours befasst sich mit großen Epidemien wie dem afrikanischen Ebola-Ausbruch im Sudan, der schließlich zu Fällen in den Vereinigten Staaten und England führte, dem indischen Pockenausbruch in Bihar, dem afrikanischen Cholera-Ausbruch, dem SARS-Ausbruch in Guanghzhou, China, und der Vogelgrippe in Hongkong.

programme.title: Desperate Hours - S01:E04 - Wind
programme.desc: Desperate Hours befasst sich mit der zerstörerischen Kraft des Windes. Von der verheerenden Kraft des Hurrikans Katrina in New Orleans über den zerstörerischen Zyklon Nargis in Myanmar, den Sturm Hurrikan Donna von 1960 in Florida, den Tornado Joplin in Missouri und den Hurrikan Sandy in New York.

programme.title: Desperate Hours - S01:E05 - Fire
programme.desc: Der lodernde Schrecken eines Feuers ist an Bedrohung kaum zu überbieten. Desperate Hours befasst sich mit der zerstörerischen Kraft von Bränden, darunter die Buschbrände vom Schwarzen Samstag in Victoria (Australien), das Feuer im Station Nightclub in Rhode Island, das Feuer im Kaufhaus L'Innovation in Brüssel (Belgien), das Feuer im Zeughaus in Lagos (Nigeria) und das Feuer in der Binhai New Development Zone in Tianjin (China).

programme.title: Desperate Hours - S01:E06 - Flood
programme.desc: Wenn der Deich bricht, geht es erst richtig los.... Desperate Hours befasst sich mit einigen der verheerendsten Überschwemmungen der Geschichte, darunter die Überschwemmung von Rio de Janeiro in Brasilien, die Überschwemmungen in Louisiana in den Vereinigten Staaten, die Überschwemmungen in Hamburg in Deutschland, die Überschwemmungen in Uttarakhand in Nordindien und der Tsunami im Indischen Ozean in Südostasien.

programme.title: The Trail - S01:E01 - Into the High Lonesome
programme.desc: Laramy Sasquatch Miller bricht mit seinem treuen Pferd 7 auf, um seine Überlebensfähigkeiten zu testen und den Bergmännern der Vergangenheit zu huldigen. Im Abschied von seiner Familie müssen Laramy und 7 den Elementen trotzen, Unterkünfte bauen, vom Land leben und eine 30-tägige Wanderung durch die Wildnis überleben.

programme.title: The Trail - S01:E02 - Camp Otis
programme.desc: Der moderne Bergmann Laramy Miller macht sich auf den Weg, um das Leben in der einsamen Höhe zu erleben.

programme.title: The Trail- S01:E03 - Relocation
programme.desc: Laramy erkennt, dass Wild rar ist wegen der Wölfe und beschließt, etwas Abstand zwischen sich und dem Rudel zu schaffen. Zeit, das Lager abzubrechen und an einen neuen Ort zu ziehen.

programme.title: The Trail - S01:E04 - White Death
programme.desc: Schnee, den die Bergmenschen weißer Tod nannten, stoppt Laramy in seiner Spur. Zudem hat sein treues Pferd Sieben eine Beinverletzung erlitten. Laramy muss sich um Sieben kümmern, den Hunger bekämpfen und den Schneefall überleben.

programme.title: Dropped - S03:E09 - Bull In The Bush
programme.desc: Nach mehr als drei Wochen Wandern durch Elchland schlagen die Brüder Chris und Casey Keefer endlich ein Lager an einem neuen Ort auf und stehen fast sofort einem gewaltigen Bullen gegenüber.

programme.title: Dropped - S03:E10 - End Of The Line

programme.desc: Laramy Sasquatch Miller bricht mit seinem treuen Pferd 7 auf, um seine Überlebensfähigkeiten zu testen und den Bergmännern der Vergangenheit zu huldigen. Im Abschied von seiner Familie müssen Laramy und 7 den Elementen trotzen, Unterkünfte bauen, vom Land leben und eine 30-tägige Wanderung durch die Wildnis überleben.

programme.title: The Trail - S01:E02 - Camp Otis
programme.desc: Der moderne Bergmann Laramy Miller macht sich auf den Weg, um das Leben in der einsamen Höhe zu erleben.

programme.title: The Trail- S01:E03 - Relocation
programme.desc: Laramy erkennt, dass Wild rar ist wegen der Wölfe und beschließt, etwas Abstand zwischen sich und dem Rudel zu schaffen. Zeit, das Lager abzubrechen und an einen neuen Ort zu ziehen.

programme.title: The Trail - S01:E04 - White Death
programme.desc: Schnee, den die Bergmenschen weißer Tod nannten, stoppt Laramy in seiner Spur. Zudem hat sein treues Pferd Sieben eine Beinverletzung erlitten. Laramy muss sich um Sieben kümmern, den Hunger bekämpfen und den Schneefall überleben.

programme.title: Dropped - S03:E09 - Bull In The Bush
programme.desc: Nach mehr als drei Wochen Wandern durch Elchland schlagen die Brüder Chris und Casey Keefer endlich ein Lager an einem neuen Ort auf und stehen fast sofort einem gewaltigen Bullen gegenüber.

programme.title: Dropped - S03:E10 - End Of The Line
programme.desc: Mit den Chancen gegen sie ist das 80-Meilen-Flussabenteuer fast vorbei, und die Brüder Chris und Casey Keefer haben eine letzte Gelegenheit, einen Alaskamoose nur wenige Meilen von dem Ort zu finden, an dem sie an der Flusskreuzung abgeholt werden.

programme.title: Dropped - S04:E01 - To Be Alive
programme.desc: Chris und Casey Keefer sehen sich Videoeinträge an, um ein glückliches Paar auszuwählen, das mit ihnen nach Alaska kommt. Die Jäger treffen sich zum ersten Mal am Flughafen. Casey und Kaid werden abgesetzt, während Chris und der Rest am Hangar auf Verzögerungen stoßen.

programme.title: Dropped - S04:E02 - Separation Anxiety
programme.desc: Das Wetter in Alaska wird gerade so mild, dass Chris und Kaid ihr Ziel erreichen und etwas zu essen fangen können. Währenddessen haben Casey und R.L. bereits ihr Camp eingerichtet und bereiten sich auf die Elch-Saison vor. Die Brüder müssen sich daran gewöhnen, zum ersten Mal getrennt abgesetzt zu werden.

programme.title: Dropped - S04:E03 - Predators Prowl
programme.desc: Chris und Kaid bereiteten ihre Rafts vor, während Casey und R.L. begannen, nach Schwarzbären und Elchen Ausschau zu halten. Just in diesem Moment bemerkten Chris und Kaid einen Raubtier, das sich ihrem Lagerplatz näherte.

programme.title: Desperate Hours - S01:E02 - Earthquake
programme.desc: Wenn sich die Erde wirklich bewegt, bedeutet das eine Katastrophe epischen Ausmaßes. Desperate Hours befasst sich mit der zerstörerischen Kraft von Erdbeben und betrachtet das Kathmandu-Erdbeben 2015 in Nepal, das Sichuan-Erdbeben 2008 in China, bei dem schätzungsweise 10.000 Kinder in eingestürzten Schulgebäuden eingeschlossen waren, ein Puerto-Mont-Erdbeben 1960 in Chile, das Port-au-Princ-Erdbeben 2010 in Haiti und das Kaschmir-Erdbeben 2005 in Pakistan.

programme.title: Desperate Hours - S01:E03 - Epidemic
programme.desc: Desperate Hours befasst sich mit großen Epidemien wie dem afrikanischen Ebola-Ausbruch im Sudan, der schließlich zu Fällen in den Vereinigten Staaten und England führte, dem indischen Pockenausbruch in Bihar, dem afrikanischen Cholera-Ausbruch, dem SARS-Ausbruch in Guanghzhou, China, und der Vogelgrippe in Hongkong.

programme.title: Desperate Hours - S01:E04 - Wind
programme.desc: Desperate Hours befasst sich mit der zerstörerischen Kraft des Windes. Von der verheerenden Kraft des Hurrikans Katrina in New Orleans über den zerstörerischen Zyklon Nargis in Myanmar, den Sturm Hurrikan Donna von 1960 in Florida, den Tornado Joplin in Missouri und den Hurrikan Sandy in New York.

programme.title: Desperate Hours - S01:E05 - Fire
programme.desc: Der lodernde Schrecken eines Feuers ist an Bedrohung kaum zu überbieten. Desperate Hours befasst sich mit der zerstörerischen Kraft von Bränden, darunter die Buschbrände vom Schwarzen Samstag in Victoria (Australien), das Feuer im Station Nightclub in Rhode Island, das Feuer im Kaufhaus L'Innovation in Brüssel (Belgien), das Feuer im Zeughaus in Lagos (Nigeria) und das Feuer in der Binhai New Development Zone in Tianjin (China).

programme.title: Desperate Hours - S01:E06 - Flood
programme.desc: Wenn der Deich bricht, geht es erst richtig los.... Desperate Hours befasst sich mit einigen der verheerendsten Überschwemmungen der Geschichte, darunter die Überschwemmung von Rio de Janeiro in Brasilien, die Überschwemmungen in Louisiana in den Vereinigten Staaten, die Überschwemmungen in Hamburg in Deutschland, die Überschwemmungen in Uttarakhand in Nordindien und der Tsunami im Indischen Ozean in Südostasien.

programme.title: The Trail - S01:E01 - Into the High Lonesome

programme.title: Royal Documentaries - S01:E17 - Remembering Diana Princess Of Wales
programme.desc: Erinnerung an Diana, Prinzessin von Wales zeigt Szenen von ihrer Staatbegräbnis und herzliche Worte von ihren engsten Freunden. Es ist ein warmer und nachdenklicher Blick auf Diana sowohl als Person als auch als öffentliche Figur: die Königin, die niemals war und die Prinzessin, die für immer in Erinnerung bleiben wird.

programme.title: Royal Documentaries - S01:E22 - Harry The Mysterious Prince
programme.desc: Trotz des ganzen Medienrummels, der vieles anderes als schmeichelhaft ist, bleibt Harry ein bisschen ein Rätsel. Diese fesselnde Dokumentation bietet frische Einblicke und Aufnahmen, um einen königlichen Prinzen zu beleuchten, der sowohl mit Herzschmerz als auch mit Skandalen zu kämpfen hatte.

programme.title: Dropped - S08:E05 - Bull Run
programme.desc: Wenn die Sonne aufgeht, erweckt sie das Tal zum Leben, und die Tiere beginnen sich zu rühren und zu bewegen.

programme.title: Dropped - S08:E06 - Stay Or Go
programme.desc: Es gibt noch einen langen Weg auf der Reise, aber sollten die Brüder Tiere loslassen, um nach anderen Tieren zu suchen?

programme.title: Wardens - S05:E18 - Head Case
programme.desc: Jemand erschießt illegal einen prächtigen Trophäen-Elch und versteckt dessen Kopf. Wildhüter beobachten das Gebiet monatelang. Als der Kopf schließlich bewegt wird, nutzen sie fortschrittliche Technologie, um ihn zu verfolgen und die verantwortliche Person zu fassen.

programme.title: Wardens - S05:E19 - Blood Trail
programme.desc: Wildhüter betreten die Welt von CSI, um zu bestimmen, wo ein Hirsch getötet wurde; wenn man die Wahrheit aufdeckt, ist es am besten, der Blutspur zu folgen.

programme.title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots - S02:E06 - Into The Deep
programme.desc: Die Buschpiloten müssen ihr ganzes Können einsetzen - Tauchen, Unterwassertakelage und Expertenflug - um einen de Havilland Beaver-Flügel auf dem Grund des 6 Mile Lake zu bergen.

programme.title: Alaska's Ultimate Bush Pilots - S02:E07 - Flying High
programme.desc: Die Buschpiloten von Tanalian bringen extreme Gleitschirmflieger nach Moose Creek in der Talkeetna Range, wo sie eine einmalige Erfahrung in der Luft machen.

programme.title: Gold Fever - S20:E05 - Burnt River
programme.desc: Diese Woche bei Gold Fever sind Tom und seine Tochter Kia in Ost-Oregon auf der Suche nach Gold.

programme.title: Gold Fever - S20:E06 - Golden Flight
programme.desc: Diese Woche bei Gold Fever sind Tom und sein Sohn Kael in Anchorage beim Saugbaggern, um etwas Gold für Cindys Geburtstagsgeschenk zu finden.

programme.title: Royal Documentaries - S01:E25 - Kate The Making Of A Modern Queen
programme.desc: A look at the Duchess of Cambridge's journey from being an everyday young woman to becoming a future queen, and what her evolution signifies for the royal family.

programme.title: Royal Documentaries - S01:E17 - Remembering Diana Princess Of Wales

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! ARC-V
programme.desc: It could be curtains for Yuya when a rival student cons him out of his Pendulum Cards!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! ARC-V
programme.desc: Still shocked by Sylvio's Pendulum Summon, Yuya must find his focus if he wants to save his friends from a 100-story drop!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! ARC-V
programme.desc: The new kid in town drives Yuya bananas when he begs Yuya to be his duel teacher! Unfortunately for Yuya, this blue-haired battler won't give up until Yuya gives in!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! ARC-V
programme.desc: Yuya's getting schooled by his wannabe student, Sora! Can Yuya turn things around and teach Sora a lesson about winning, or is Yuya's Pendulum power not going to make the grade?

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! ARC-V
programme.desc: Still steaming from his loss to Yuya, Sylvio looks to get some payback! But when Zuzu steps in to stop his plans, a mysterious stranger comes out of the shadows to show her how it's done!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! ARC-V
programme.desc: The Leo Institute is looking to take over Yuya's duel school, and they're bringing 3 of their most dangerous duelists to take down whoever dares stand in their way!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! ARC-V
